sanskrit:
शुक्लकृष्णे गते ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते।
एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययावर्तते पुनः॥
transliteration: śukla-kṛṣṇe gate hy ete jagataḥ śāśvate mate | ekayā yāty anāvṛttim anyayāvartate punaḥ ||
literal_meaning: By the bright and by the dark path these two are certainly considered the eternal paths of the world; by one he goes to non-return, by the other he returns again.
contextual_meaning: The psyche faces a decisive fork: one path (light) symbolizes ego-transcendence and permanent integration; the other (dark) loops back into repetitive cycles of craving and rebirth.
psychological_state: Pre-decisional arousal—mind weighing the terror of permanent change against the exhaustion of cyclic suffering.
trigger_scenario: Standing at the edge of quitting a toxic job or relationship: one road leads to an unknown but irreversible new life, the other back into the same draining loop.
anchor_text: Choose the path of light—let go of ego and never return to suffering.
